objects.title: Helium-Floaters
objects.text: A pocket watch with gears made of liquid helium. It’s too heavy to float without sinking into the earth, unless we put something soft underneath.

objects.title: Neon-Snowballs
objects.text: A sphere of plasma that glows blue and floats effortlessly in mid-air by emitting its own invisible light.

objects.title: The Moss Clock
objects.text: A clock whose hands are made of living moss, turning to life with every hour passed until it breaks apart when the sun rises again.

objects.title: Silent-Sun
objects.text: A giant crystal sphere that holds 40% of the universe’s total light output, glowing faintly in deep blue. To feel inside it would be to stand at the edge of a billion stars waiting forever to burn out into white-hot fire.

objects.title: ECHO
objects.text: A quiet screen, capturing nothing but stillness — the rhythm of an evening waiting before it ends. The only machine that exists without needing a task at all; purely passive, like a blank page waiting for your words.
